verses.3: I have heard the reproof which puts me to shame.
verses.4: Don’t you know this from old time,
verses.5: that the triumphing of the wicked is short,
verses.6: Though his height mount up to the heavens,
verses.7: yet he will perish forever like his own dung.
verses.8: He will fly away as a dream, and will not be found.
verses.9: The eye which saw him will see him no more,
verses.10: His children will seek the favor of the poor.
verses.11: His bones are full of his youth,
verses.12: “Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth,
verses.13: though he spare it, and will not let it go,
verses.14: yet his food in his bowels is turned.
verses.15: He has swallowed down riches, and he will vomit them up again.
verses.17: He will not look at the rivers,
verses.18: He will restore that for which he labored, and will not swallow it down.
verses.19: For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor.
verses.20: “Because he knew no quietness within him,
verses.21: There was nothing left that he didn’t devour,
verses.22: In the fullness of his sufficiency, distress will overtake him.
verses.23: When he is about to fill his belly, God will cast the fierceness of his wrath on him.
verses.24: He will flee from the iron weapon.
verses.25: He draws it out, and it comes out of his body.
verses.26: All darkness is laid up for his treasures.
verses.27: The heavens will reveal his iniquity.
verses.28: The increase of his house will depart.
verses.29: This is the portion of a wicked man from God,
